name: Black Coral
desc: These deep-water organisms possess a distinct dark, thorny skeleton often used for jewelry.

name: Sea Whip Pen
desc: A genus of slender, whip-like sea pens that inhabit soft muddy bottoms in deep-sea environments.

name: Bright Sea Fan
desc: Vibrant sea fans characterized by intricate, colorful branching patterns often found in tropical and subtropical reefs.

name: Sea Whip
desc: These gorgonians have flexible, slender branches that sway with the ocean currents.

name: Soft Coral
desc: A broad subclass of corals (including soft corals and sea fans) defined by their eight-fold radial symmetry.

name: Bubblegum Coral
desc: Known for its bulbous, colorful tips; it forms large, tree-like structures in the deep sea.

name: Sea Pen
desc: A group of colonial cnidarians, which anchor themselves into soft sediment using a fleshy bulb.

name: Feather Coral
desc: Delicate, fan-shaped gorgonians with fine, feathery branches typically found in deep, cold waters.

name: Fleshy Sea Pen
desc: These orange-hued organisms resemble large, stout feathers.

name: Needle Sea Pen
desc: Very slender, needle-like sea pens that can retract into the sand when disturbed by predators or strong flows.
